short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · next earnings cycle)
bullets: Run-rate $1.01B in FY2026 Q1, +32.2% YoY — accelerating trajectory entering the next print.
Next-quarter consensus: $0.91B (-9.9% vs current Q) — set by 30 broker contributors.
EPS consensus next qtr: $0.50 vs current $0.15.
Margin context: 5.2% net margin — compressed — watch operating leverage.
Next earnings: 2026-08-05 — primary catalyst for the short-term window.
short_term.takeaway: Going into the next earnings print, the setup is mixed — execution risk on the next guide is elevated.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · multi-year setup)
bullets: TTM revenue $4B — in cyclical drawdown.
Sell-side: FY1 $4B → FY2 $4B (+21.4% multi-year top-line implied).
Forward EBIT margin: 28.0% — structural premium business over the long-cycle view.
7-quarter run-rate CAGR: +34.5% — the secular trajectory the market is pricing.
Sector thesis (Information Technology): Multi-year AI/cloud capex cycle remains the dominant secular theme.
long_term.takeaway: Long-cycle thesis is intact if the next 2–4 quarters confirm a stabilization in trajectory.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–45 DTE · front-month tactical)
bullets: Front-month ATM IV: 69.3% — elevated (premium-selling favored).
Put/Call OI: 1.13 — balanced flow.
30-day implied move: ±14.7% ($180.99 – $243.49) — the 1-σ range the options market is pricing.
ATM skew: -7.72pp — call skew (upside bid — bullish positioning).
Front-month OI cluster: $150.0 — likely magnet / pin-risk for expiry.
short_term.strategy: Sell 30–45 DTE cash-secured put at $202 (5% OTM). High IV (69%) + uptrend → collect premium with a willingness to be assigned at a discount. Roll if breached, otherwise let theta decay.

long_term.strategy: Sell 6-month strangle: short $191 put / short $233 call. Range-bound + high IV → premium-selling on both sides; profits if spot stays within ±10%; manage with rolls or assignment.
